retry_reason: Put operations are release operations; retrying would lead to double-put and a use-after-free risk.
contract_summary: This wrapper releases one reference to a struct l2cap_chan by calling l2cap_clear_timer, which decrements the kref refcount (via chan.kref.refcount). It is used to drop the reference held by a pending ack timer. After this call, the caller must not access the channel if this was the last reference, as the channel may be freed. The wrapper is unconditional; the caller must ensure that the channel has a valid reference to release (i.e., the timer was previously armed and a reference was taken). There is no return value to check.

judge.reason: The macro's sole purpose is to call l2cap_clear_timer, which is a put function on the channel's kref refcount. No other logic exists; it is a pure reference-count release wrapper.

check.reason: The macro __clear_ack_timer calls l2cap_clear_timer(c, &c->ack_timer), which is a put function on the first parameter c. The access path c.kref.refcount correctly traces from the parameter to the refcount field. The segment count matches the type chain (3 segments, 3 types). No inconsistencies found.
